title: Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary is a high-altitude protected area in the Indian Himalayas.
overview.description: Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary is a high-altitude protected area in the Indian Himalayas. It is located in the Spiti Valley of Lahaul and Spiti district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It was established in 1999, and covers an area of 2,220.12 km2 (857.19 sq mi). It is the only cold desert sanctuary in India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kaza, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Kaza, also spelled Kaze, Karze, Karzey, is a town and the subdivisional headquarters of the remote Spiti Valley in the western Himalayas in the Lahaul and Spiti district of the northern Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Spiti is a high altitude or cold desert having close similarities to the neighbouring Tibet and Ladakh regions in terms of terrain, climate and the Buddhist culture.

topPlaces.name: Hikkim
topPlaces.description: Hikkim is a village in Lahaul and Spiti district in the northern Indian state of Himachal Pradesh at an elevation of 4,400 m (14,400 ft). It is 15 km (9.3 mi) from Kaza, the nearest town connected by road.

topPlaces.name: Komic
topPlaces.description: Komic (also spelled as Komik) is a small village located in Spiti Tehsil of Lahaul and Spiti district of Himachal Pradesh with a population of 130, out of which 90 are males and 40 are females as per the India Population Census of 2011. Komic village is one of the world's highest motorable villages.

topPlaces.name: Tangyud Monastery
topPlaces.description: The Tangyud Monastery (also written bTang-rGyud, Tangyuth) or Sa-skya-gong-mig Gompa at the village of Komic, two km southeast of Hikkim in the Spiti Valley of Himachal Pradesh, India, is built like a fortified castle on the edge of a deep canyon, with massive slanted mud walls and battlements with vertical red ochre and white vertical stripes which make them look much taller than they really are. It is one of the highest altitude gompas (monasteries) in India, at an altitude of 4,520 metres (14,830 ft), on the edge of a deep canyon and overlooking the town of Kaza, Spiti 4 km to the west.

topPlaces.name: Spiti

topPlaces.name: Langza
topPlaces.description: Langza is a small village located in Spiti Tehsil of Lahaul and Spiti district, Himachal Pradesh. It is set at the base of Chau Chau Kang Nilda mountain, also known as the Princess Mountain.

Komic village is one of the world's highest motorable villages.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (208 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kaza, Himachal Pradesh, Hikkim, Komic, Tangyud Monastery, Spiti.
